LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT FINANCIAL FREEDOM SENIOR FUNDING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OF PLAINTIFF DIAMOND HEIGHTS VILLAGE ASSOCIATION'S COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF FAILS TO CREATE A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT. AFTER PLAINTIFF OBTAINED THE JUDGMENT IN THE FIRST ACTION, IT DID NOT RECORD THAT JUDGMENT TO PROTECT ITS PRIORITY. PLAINTIFF'S LIENS MERGED INTO THE JUDGMENT AND DEFENDANT RECORDED ITS INTEREST FIRST. CCP SECTION 697.020 DOES NOT APPLY TO THE INSTANT CASE. SUBSECTION (A) APPLIES TO ATTACHMENT LIENS AND SUBSECTION (B) APPLIES TO LIENS CREATED UNDER A DISCRETE SECTION OF THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, NOT CIVIL CODE SECTION 1367. (THE COURT RULINGS ON EVIDENTIARY OBJECTION AS FOLLOWS: AS TO FINANCIAL FREEDOM, OVERRULED AS TO #1, AND THE REMAINDER ARE SUSTAINED; AS TO DIAMOND HEIGHTS, OVERRULED AS TO #1, AND THE REMAINDER ARE SUSTAINED.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Notice Of Motion To Vacate Judgment
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Rodrigo Dias, Esq. on behalf of Diamond Heights Village Assoc.
Carlos Alvarez, Esq. and Benjamin Ehrhart, Esq. on behalf of Financial Freedom

Court hears arguments by respective counsel as to Financial Freedom's Motion to Vacate Judgment and Plaintiff's Motion for Attorney's Fees & Costs.

Both sides to submit a proposed order to the Court as to each of the motions by November 13, 2009.

(Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Attorney'S Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Oct-01-2009 Continued To Nov-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 18

MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
Off Calendar Oct-01-2009 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FINANCIAL FREEDOM SENIOR FUNDING CORPORATION, Motion for Attorneys Fees and Costs. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion denied. Review of Judge Davis's decision and the supplemental Briefs does not alter the court's tentative decision. There is no privity between plaintiff and defendant and the case concerned priority. Clar vs. Cacciola (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 1032 is the controlling authority. No basis for recovery under section 1367. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Aug-21-2009 Continued To Aug-27-09 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Dias, Esq. of Ragghianti/Freitas LLP (415) 453-9433 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Carlos A. Alvarez, of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith (415) 421-3400, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the matter is continued to August 27, 2009 for further hearing. (Any further briefing is due by close of business 8/25/09, five pages of less.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR 5797

Off Calendar Jun-26-2009 Text Ruling

Off Calendar May-26-2009 Assigned To Trial Court

COURT TRIAL
Off Calendar May-29-2009 Continued To Jun-02-09 At 08:30 A.M. In Dept 306

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-08-2009 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar May-06-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FINANCIAL FREEDOM SENIOR FUNDING COMPANY'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT of Plaintiff Diamond Heights Village Association's complaint. Appearances: David F. Feingold, of Ragghianti Freitas LLP (415) 453-9433, appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Carlos A. Alvarez, Esq. and Benjamin R. Ehrhart, Esq. of Steyer Lowenthal Boodrookas Alvarez & Smith (415) 421-3400, appearing for Defendant and moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff fails to create a triable issue of fact. After Plaintiff obtained the judgment in the first action, it did not record that judgment to protect its priority. Plaintiff's liens merged into the judgment and defendant recorded its interest first. CCP section 697.020 does not apply to the instant case. Subsection (a) applies to attachment liens and subsection (b) applies to liens created under a discrete section of the Code of Civil Procedure, not Civil Code section 1367. (The Court rulings on Evidentiary Objection as follows: as to Financial Freedom, overruled as to #1, and the remainder are sustained; as to Diamond Heights, overruled as to #1, and the remainder are sustained.) Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW/PB)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5759
